title: 10 best herbal creams for dry skin

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe creams helps with dry skin because they provide an intense moisturizing experience, locking in moisture to soothe and calm parched skin. The aloe vera gel in these creams contains natural anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness and irritation, while its humectant properties attract and retain moisture from the air. This combination of nourishment and hydration helps to repair and restore dry skin's natural barrier function, leaving it soft, supple, and feeling refreshed.
remedy_properties: Moisturizing Properties: Herbal aloe creams contain polysaccharides, which help to lock in moisture and soothe dry, itchy skin by forming a protective barrier on the skin's surface.

Anti-inflammatory Effects: The bioactive compounds present in herbal aloe creams, such as aloin and aloe-emodin, have anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce redness, swelling, and discomfort associated with dry skin.

Antioxidant Properties: Herbal aloe creams contain antioxidants like vitamin E and beta-carotene, which help to neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and damage to the skin, leading to dryness and premature aging.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in vitamins, minerals, and amino acids, making them an excellent source of natural moisturizers and emollients.
Buds: Containing high amounts of anthraquinones, which help soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing inflammation and promoting healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality aloe vera leaves and rinse them gently under cool running water.
Remove the thick green rind from the aloe vera leaves using a sharp knife or vegetable peeler.
Extract the clear gel from the aloe vera leaves by cutting them into small pieces and blending with 1 cup of distilled water for 5 minutes.
Mix the extracted aloe vera gel with 1/2 cup of coconut oil, 1 tablespoon of beeswax, and 1 teaspoon of vitamin e oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture under low heat stirring constantly until it reaches a temperature of 120°f to 140°f for about 10 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold creams helps with dry skin because of its unique ability to lock in moisture and soothe irritated skin. The herb's anti-inflammatory properties reduce redness and discomfort, while its rich antioxidants work to repair and regenerate the skin's natural barrier. Additionally, pot marigold contains omega-6 fatty acids that deeply moisturize and hydrate the skin, leaving it feeling soft, supple, and nourished. As a result, pot marigold creams provide long-lasting relief from dry, flaky skin, promoting healthy and radiant complexion.
remedy_properties: Emollient property: Pot marigold cream's emollient property helps to lock in moisture and soothe dry, irritated skin by creating a protective barrier that prevents water loss.

Anti-inflammatory property: The anti-inflammatory compound calophyllolide present in pot marigold has been shown to reduce redness and swelling associated with dry skin conditions, making it an effective treatment for eczema and other inflammatory skin issues.

Antioxidant property: Pot marigold's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and damage to the skin, contributing to signs of aging such as fine lines, wrinkles, and dryness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, roots help soothe and calm dry skin.
Flowers: Petals are high in vitamins A and E, which nourish and moisturize dry skin, reducing inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Gather one cup of dried calendula flowers, two tablespoons of coconut oil and four ounces of beeswax pellets for preparation.
Infuse the dried calendula flowers in the coconut oil by heating it gently over low heat for 30 minutes.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove the calendula flowers, discarding the solids.
Melt the beeswax pellets and combine them with the infused oil, stirring constantly until they are fully incorporated.
Pour the mixture into a glass jar and let it cool and solidify at room temperature for 30 minutes before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile creams helps with dry skin because of its soothing and moisturizing properties. The herb's anti-inflammatory compounds calm and quiet irritated skin, reducing redness and itching associated with dryness. Additionally, chamomile's emollient properties help to lock in moisture, leaving the skin feeling soft and supple. As a natural humectant, it also attracts and retains water, helping to deeply hydrate the skin and provide long-lasting relief from dryness and flakiness.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with dry skin, promoting a more comfortable and calm complexion.

Antioxidant properties: Chamomile's antioxidants neutralize free radicals that can damage skin cells and accelerate aging, protecting the skin from environmental stressors and maintaining its natural moisture barrier.
Soothing and emollient properties: Chamomile's oils and extracts have a calming effect on the skin, locking in moisture and providing long-lasting hydration to dry, parched skin.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which help to soothe and calm dry skin.
Flowers: Contain apigenin, an flavonoid that helps to reduce inflammation and promote skin hydration.
remedy_recipe: Harvest matricaria chamomilla flowers, drying them completely within 24 hours.
Combine 20% dried chamomilla flowers with 80% carrier oil such as sweet almond oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture gently over medium heat for 30 minutes to allow the herbs to infuse into the oil.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove solids within 10-15 minutes.
Whip the strained oil with beeswax and vitamin e oil to create a creamy texture, taking approximately 10-15 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender creams helps with dry skin because of its soothing and moisturizing properties. The cream's active ingredients, such as beta-lactones, callophyllolide, and camphor, work together to create a protective barrier on the skin's surface, locking in moisture and reducing water loss. Additionally, the calming effects of lavender oil help to reduce inflammation and irritation, leaving dry and flaky skin feeling soft, smooth, and comfortable.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Lavender oil's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce redness, swelling, and discomfort associated with dry skin, making it more comfortable to live with.
Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in lavender oil protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate dry skin and lead to premature aging.

Moisturizing and soothing properties: Lavender oil's emollient and humectant properties help lock in moisture, soothe irritation, and calm dry, itchy skin, leaving it feeling soft, smooth, and supple.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, lavender roots help soothe and calm dry skin.
Flowers: Lavender flowers are a rich source of fatty acids and flavonoids, which provide moisturizing and anti-aging benefits to dry skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry high-quality lavender flowers lavandula angustifolia in a warm, dark place for several weeks.
Combine dried lavender flowers with sweet almond oil and beeswax pellets in a double boiler containing 250ml water.
Heat the mixture to 160°f over low heat while stirring occasionally until beeswax is fully melted and combined with oil.
Remove from heat and allow the mixture to cool slightly before whipping it with an electric mixer for 5-7 minutes until thickened.
Pour the cream into sterilized glass jars, seal tightly, and store in a cool dark place for up to 6 months or refrigerate for up to 2 weeks.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow creams helps with dry skin because they are rich in natural emollients that deeply moisturize and nourish the skin. The saponins present in marshmallows create a protective barrier on the skin's surface, locking in moisture and reducing water loss. Additionally, marshmallow creams often contain other soothing herbs like chamomile and calendula, which further calm and comfort dry, irritated skin, leaving it feeling soft, supple, and comfortable. This natural blend of ingredients makes them an effective treatment for dry skin.
remedy_properties: Emollient properties: Marshmallow creams contain mucilage, a natural thickening agent that forms a protective barrier on the skin's surface, locking in moisture and soothing dryness. This property helps to calm and hydrate dry skin.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Marshmallow creams have anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce redness, swelling, and irritation associated with dry skin, making it an effective treatment for conditions like eczema and dermatitis.

Protective barrier function: The mucilage in marshmallow creams forms a protective film on the skin's surface, shielding it from environmental stressors, wind, and cold weather that can exacerbate dryness, while also helping to retain moisture and protect the skin's natural barrier.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in mucilage, the roots provide a soothing and protective barrier for dry skin, locking in moisture and reducing irritation.
Rhyzomes: Containing high amounts of saponins, rhyzomes help to gently cleanse and moisturize dry skin, while also providing anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality dried marshmallow root powder, distilled water, glycerin, and petroleum jelly.
Weigh out 50 grams of dried marshmallow root powder and mix with 200 milliliters of distilled water in a heat-resistant container.
Heat the mixture on low for 20 minutes, stirring occasionally, to create a soothing herbal infusion.
Strain the infusion through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another container, discarding the solids and retaining the liquid.
Combine the strained liquid with 30 grams of glycerin and 50 grams of petroleum jelly, whipping until smooth and creamy.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Geranium creams helps with dry skin because it contains natural emollients that deeply moisturize and nourish the skin. The herb's sweet, floral scent also has a soothing effect on irritated skin, reducing inflammation and redness. As the cream is absorbed, it forms a protective barrier on the skin's surface, locking in moisture and preventing further dryness. Additionally, geranium's anti-inflammatory properties help to calm and comfort dry, itchy skin, leaving it feeling soft, smooth, and refreshed.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Geranium essential oil has antioxidant properties that help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to dryness and wrinkles.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in geranium cream can help reduce redness and irritation associated with dry skin, promoting a more comfortable and balanced skin tone.
Emollient properties: Geranium cream's emollient properties help lock in moisture, reducing the appearance of fine lines and wrinkles while leaving the skin feeling soft, supple, and hydrated.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the geranium plant contain compounds that help to soothe and calm irritated skin, making them an excellent choice for relieving dry, itchy skin.
Buds: Geranium buds are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce redness and inflammation associated with dry skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh or dried pelargonium graveolens leaves in the early morning for optimal essential oil content and aroma.
Combine 20 grams of dried pelargonium graveolens leaves with 200 milliliters of carrier oil such as sweet almond or coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture gently over low heat for 30 minutes to allow for infusion and solvent extraction of the herb's beneficial compounds and essential oils.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass container discarding the solids and preserving the infused carrier oil.

Allow the infused carrier oil to cool and solidify at room temperature for approximately 30 minutes before whipping it with an electric mixer until it reaches the desired consistency of a rich moisturizing cream.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree creams helps with dry skin because they contain unique compounds found in the leaves of the maidenhair tree, specifically Ginkgo biloba. These compounds have been shown to deeply moisturize and nourish dry skin, while also providing antioxidant protection against environmental stressors that can exacerbate dryness. By incorporating these natural extracts into creams, they help to lock in moisture, soothe itchiness, and promote a healthy barrier function, leaving dry skin feeling soft, supple, and hydrated.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Antioxidants help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to signs of aging and dryness.
Moisturizing and emollient properties: Some plant extracts with moisturizing properties can help lock in moisture and soothe dry, irritated skin.
Anti-inflammatory effects: Reducing inflammation can help alleviate symptoms of dry skin, such as itching, redness, and scaling.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the maidenhair tree contain saponins, which have emollient properties and help to soothe and moisturize dry skin.
Barks: The barks of the maidenhair tree are rich in flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce redness and irritation associated with dry skin.
remedy_recipe: Measure 100g of high-quality beeswax and 200g of coconut oil into a heat-resistant mixing bowl.
Add 50ml of distilled water and 10ml of glycerin to the mixture, stirring until the beeswax is fully melted.
Combine 20g of dried ginkgo biloba extract with 40g of sweet almond oil in a separate container, letting it infuse for 2 hours.
Slowly add the infused ginkgo biloba mixture to the beeswax and coconut oil blend, stirring until well combined and cooled to 60°c.
Pour the mixture into sterilized jars and let it set at room temperature for 30 minutes before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tea tree creams helps with dry skin because of its exceptional emollient properties, which deeply moisturize and nourish the skin. The cream's anti-inflammatory compounds soothe and calm dry, itchy patches, reducing redness and discomfort. Additionally, tea tree oil's natural antimicrobial abilities help to prevent bacterial infections that can exacerbate dry skin conditions like eczema and dermatitis. By providing long-lasting hydration and protection, tea tree creams effectively alleviate dry skin symptoms, leaving skin feeling soft, smooth, and revitalized.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial activity: Tea tree oil, a key ingredient in many herbal tea tree creams, has antimicrobial properties that help prevent bacterial and fungal infections that can exacerbate dry skin.
Anti-inflammatory effects: The cream's anti-inflammatory properties can reduce redness, itching, and swelling associated with dry skin, promoting a calm and comfortable complexion.
Moisturizing and emollient properties: Many herbal tea tree creams contain moisturizing and emollient ingredients that help lock in hydration, soften dry patches, and soothe dry, rough skin.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Known for their high concentration of melaleuca oil, which has antifungal and antibacterial properties that help soothe and calm dry, irritated skin.
Roots: Used to create a rich, moisturizing cream that can lock in moisture and reduce inflammation associated with dry skin conditions.
remedy_recipe: Weigh out 200 grams of distilled water and heat it in a double boiler to melt 100 grams of beeswax.
Add 20 grams of sweet almond oil and 10 grams of coconut oil to the melted beeswax mixture and stir until well combined.
Combine 50 grams of dried melaleuca alternifolia leaves with 200 milliliters of boiling water in a heat-proof cup and steep for 30 minutes.
Strain the tea through a cheesecloth into the melted oil mixture, adding 5 grams of vitamin e oil and 2 grams of lanolin powder.
Pour the mixture into sterilized jars and allow it to cool and set at room temperature for at least two hours before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Comfrey creams helps with dry skin because of its rich nutrient profile, particularly its high concentration of allantoin, a potent humectant that attracts and retains moisture. Allantoin works to lock in hydration, soothing and calming dry, flaky skin, while also promoting cell turnover and regeneration. Additionally, comfrey's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce redness and irritation associated with dry skin, leaving the skin feeling soft, supple, and comfortable. This natural cream provides long-lasting relief for parched skin, restoring its natural balance and radiance.
remedy_properties: Allantoin: Comfrey cream's allantoin content helps to lock in moisture, soothe dryness, and reduce inflammation, promoting healthy skin hydration.
Glucosamine: Glucosamine in comfrey cream acts as a natural humectant, drawing in moisture from the air to deeply hydrate and soften dry skin.
N-Acetylglucosamine: This amino sugar compound helps to repair and rebuild damaged skin tissue, reducing dryness and improving skin elasticity.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in allantoin, a natural ingredient that promotes skin regeneration and helps to repair damaged skin cells, making it ideal for soothing and moisturizing dry skin.
Rhyzomes: High in rosmarinic acid, an antioxidant compound that provides anti-inflammatory properties, helping to calm and protect dry, irritated skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest symphytum officinale leaves and flowers in their natural habitat during peak blooming season ensuring freshness and potency of the ingredients.
Dry the harvested plant material using a food dehydrator on low heat for 24 hours or air drying for 7 days to remove excess moisture.
Combine dried plant material with 20% by weight of sweet almond oil and 5% by weight of beeswax in a double boiler slowly heating the mixture over 30 minutes.
Allow the infused oil and wax mixture to cool and solidify completely at room temperature taking approximately 2 hours for a smooth consistency.
Transfer the cooled cream into sterilized glass jars filling them up to the brim and labeling with the date and ingredients used for optimal shelf life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage creams helps with dry skin because of its remarkable ability to lock in moisture and soothe irritation. The antioxidants and essential oils present in sage have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce redness and discomfort caused by dry skin. Additionally, sage's natural moisturizing agents penetrate deeply into the skin, providing long-lasting hydration and elasticity. As a result, sage creams leave dry skin feeling soft, supple, and refreshed, making them an effective solution for combating dry, flaky skin.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant Properties: Herbal sage creams contain antioxidants that help neutralize free radicals, which can damage skin cells and exacerbate dryness, reducing inflammation and promoting healthy skin.

Anti-inflammatory Properties: Sage contains salvenic acid, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties, which helps to soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness and discomfort associated with dry skin.
Moisturizing Properties: Herbal sage creams often contain fatty acids and other moisturizing compounds that help lock in moisture, leaving skin feeling soft, supple, and hydrated.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of sage contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe and calm dry, irritated skin.
Rhyzomes: Sage rhyzomes have antimicrobial properties that can help prevent infection and promote wound healing in dry, cracked skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried salvia officinalis leaves measure one ounce by weight.
Combine leaves with carrier oils such as sweet almond oil or coconut oil at a ratio of one part herb to three parts oil.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler or a heat-proof glass bowl set over simmering water for 30 minutes to allow infusion.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass jar discarding solids and discard any excess liquid.
Allow the infused oil to cool and thicken at room temperature for several hours or overnight before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme creams helps with dry skin because of its rich antioxidant and antibacterial properties. The natural oils present in thyme, such as thymol and carvacrol, have been shown to deeply moisturize and soothe dry, itchy skin. By penetrating deep into the skin's layers, these compounds help to repair and lock in moisture, leaving skin feeling soft, supple, and healthy. Additionally, thyme's anti-inflammatory properties can reduce redness and irritation, making it an effective treatment for dry skin conditions such as eczema and psoriasis.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Thyme's antibacterial compounds, such as thymol, help to combat infections and prevent further irritation and inflammation in dry, cracked skin.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in thyme, like carvacrol, reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with dry skin, promoting a soothing and calming effect.

Emollient and moisturizing properties: Thyme's natural oils and fatty acids help to lock in moisture, soften and hydrate the skin, and create a barrier against environmental stressors that can exacerbate dryness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Thyme roots contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe and calm dry, irritated skin.
Flowers: Thyme flowers are rich in vitamins A and E, which provide essential moisture and nourishment to dry skin, promoting a healthy and supple complexion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of dried thymus vulgaris leaves and stems from reputable sources or your own garden.
Combine the herb with 100 milliliters of olive oil in a double boiler set over simmering water for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container discarding solids and retaining liquid.
Add 20 grams of beeswax and 10 milliliters of coconut oil to the herbal infusion stirring until melted and combined.
Pour the cream mixture into sterilized jars filling to the top and sealing tightly for storage at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cucumber creams helps with dry skin because of their unique ability to deeply moisturize and nourish the skin. The natural oils and antioxidants present in cucumbers help to lock in moisture, reducing the appearance of fine lines and wrinkles, while also soothing and calming irritated skin. Additionally, cucumber creams are rich in vitamins A and E, which promote collagen production and improve skin elasticity, leaving dry skin feeling soft, supple, and refreshed.
remedy_properties: Moisturizing properties: Cucumber's high water content helps to lock in moisture, soothing and hydrating dry skin by creating a protective barrier against environmental stressors.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Cucumber contains anti-inflammatory compounds like fisetin and caffeic acid, which can help reduce redness and irritation caused by dry skin, promoting a calm and even tone.

Antioxidant properties: Cucumber's antioxidants, including vitamin E and beta-carotene, neutralize free radicals that can damage skin cells and cause premature aging, helping to preserve skin health and maintain a radiant glow.
remedy_parts: Roots: Cucumber roots are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in soothing and moisturizing dry skin.
Rhyzomes: Cucumber rhyzomes contain compounds that help to lock in moisture and reduce inflammation, making them ideal for hydrating and calming dry skin.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried cucumis sativus leaves and stems in amounts of one part for every four parts of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil.
Combine the cucumis sativus herb mixture with 20% glycerin and 10% beeswax in a double boiler setup over medium heat.
Stir constantly for 30 minutes to ensure the beeswax melts and the mixture reaches a temperature of 160°f to 180°f.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool to around 100°f before adding vitamin e oil at a concentration of 1% and aloe vera gel in equal parts.
Whip the cooled mixture for 10 minutes using an electric mixer or whisk until it reaches a smooth and creamy consistency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemongrass creams helps with dry skin because of its unique properties. The cream's antioxidants and anti-inflammatory agents help to soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness and flakiness. Additionally, lemongrass oil has natural moisturizing properties that lock in moisture and provide long-lasting hydration. As a result, lemongrass creams can help to soften and smoothen dry skin, leaving it feeling supple and refreshed.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Lemongrass creams contain antioxidants like vitamin C and beta-carotene, which help protect the skin from free radical damage caused by environmental stressors, thus reducing inflammation and promoting healthier, more hydrated skin.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Lemongrass has been shown to have anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce redness, itching, and irritation associated with dry skin, while also soothing and calming the skin.
Moisturizing and emollient properties: The fatty acid content in lemongrass creams helps lock in moisture, nourish, and soften dry skin, leaving it feeling supple, smooth, and hydrated.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and fatty acids, lemongrass roots are effective in moisturizing and soothing dry skin.
Rhyzomes: Containing anti-inflammatory compounds, lemongrass rhyzomes help reduce redness and irritation associated with dry skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh cymbopogon citratus leaves in the morning after dew has dried for optimal potency and freshness.
Dry the harvested leaves using a food dehydrator at 40°c for 6 hours or air drying for 24 hours.
Blend 200g of dried cymbopogon citratus leaves with 400ml of coconut oil in a double boiler at 60°c for 4 hours.
Strain the infused oil mixture through cheesecloth and squeeze out excess liquid before whipping with 20% beeswax and 10% shea butter to form a smooth cream consistency.
Package the cream in sterilized glass jars and store in the refrigerator for up to 6 months, allowing it to cool and solidify before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Grape creams helps with dry skin because they are rich in antioxidants, vitamins, and fatty acids that deeply moisturize and nourish the skin. The herbal extracts in grape creams work to repair the skin's natural barrier function, reducing water loss and leaving the skin feeling soft, supple, and hydrated. Additionally, grape creams contain anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm irritated skin, providing long-lasting relief from dryness and discomfort.
remedy_properties: Antioxidants: Herbal grape creams contain powerful antioxidants like resveratrol, which help neutralize free radicals and reduce inflammation in the skin, leading to improved hydration and reduced appearance of fine lines and wrinkles.

Emollients: Herbal grape creams typically contain emollient-rich ingredients like glycerin, panthenol, and omega-6 fatty acids, which help lock in moisture and fill gaps between skin cells, providing long-lasting relief from dryness and discomfort.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: Many herbal grape creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like salicylic acid, which help soothe irritated skin and reduce redness and itching associated with dry skin conditions.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which help soothe and moisturize dry skin.
Barks: Contains tannins that provide anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, making it effective in soothing and calming dry skin irritations.
remedy_recipe: Harvest vitis vinifera leaves and stems, wash them gently with distilled water, and chop finely.
Combine 50 grams of chopped plant material with 200 milliliters of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in a glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for four to six weeks to allow for infusion, shaking daily.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth into another container, squeezing gently to extract all liquid and discarding solids.
Mix one part of the infused oil with two parts of beeswax and heat until melted, then pour into small containers and let cool.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sweet briar creams helps with dry skin because of its unique ability to nourish and moisturize the skin. The cream's rich blend of herbal extracts, including rosehip and chamomile, work together to lock in moisture and soothe dryness, leaving the skin feeling soft, supple, and hydrated. Additionally, sweet briar's natural anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce redness and irritation, making it an effective treatment for dry, sensitive skin. Regular use of sweet briar cream can leave dry skin feeling pampered and indulged.
remedy_properties: Emollient properties: Sweet Briar cream's emollient properties help to lock in moisture and soothe dry, irritated skin, leaving it feeling soft and supple.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The herbal extracts in sweet briar cream have anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce redness and swelling associated with dry skin, promoting a more even skin tone and texture.
Antioxidant properties: Sweet Briar's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and damage to the skin, leading to dryness and premature aging.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, which help to lock in moisture and soothe dry, irritated skin.
Rhyzomes: Contain anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce redness and itching associated with dry skin.
Barks: High in flavonoids, which have antioxidant properties that protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rosa rubiginosa petals and rinse them thoroughly under cold running water for 10 minutes.
Dry the rosa rubiginosa petals in a single layer on paper towels for 24 hours or until completely dry.
Combine dried rosa rubiginosa petals with sweet almond oil and beeswax pellets in a double boiler and heat for 30 minutes.
Stir the mixture occasionally as it melts and combines into a smooth, creamy consistency taking approximately 10 minutes.
Allow the cream to cool and thicken at room temperature for 1 hour before transferring it to an airtight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric creams helps with dry skin because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm irritated skin. The curcumin present in turmeric also has moisturizing benefits, allowing it to deeply hydrate and nourish dry skin. Additionally, turmeric's antibacterial and antifungal properties help to prevent infection and promote healthy skin cell growth, further enhancing the overall appearance of dry skin. As a result, turmeric creams can effectively alleviate dryness, itchiness, and flakiness, leaving skin feeling soft, smooth, and radiant.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Turmeric's active compound curcumin reduces inflammation, which can contribute to dry skin by soothing and calming irritated skin.
Antioxidant properties: Turmeric's antioxidants neutralize free radicals that can damage skin cells and lead to dryness, promoting healthy skin cell turnover and collagen production.
Moisturizing properties: Turmeric's natural oils and fatty acids help lock in moisture, reducing water loss and leaving the skin feeling soft, supple, and hydrated.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rich in curcuminoids, rhyzomes are used to create anti-inflammatory and antioxidant-rich creams that soothe and moisturize dry skin.
Roots: Turmeric roots contain natural humectants that help retain moisture in the skin, making them effective for hydrating and nourishing dry skin.
remedy_recipe: Gather 30g of dried turmeric root curcuma longa powder and 100ml of cold-pressed coconut oil.
Combine 20g of beeswax pellets with the coconut oil in a small saucepan over low heat for 15 minutes stirring occasionally.
Add 10g of shea butter to the mixture and stir until fully incorporated and melted after 5 minutes.
Remove the saucepan from heat and let it cool slightly before adding 5g of curcuma longa powder and mixing well.
Pour the final mixture into sterilized containers and let it solidify at room temperature for 30 minutes to an hour before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice creams helps with dry skin because of its unique properties. The herb glycyrrhizin, present in licorice root, has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant qualities that soothe and calm irritated skin. Additionally, licorice extracts contain flavonoids and polyphenols that help to lock in moisture and reduce water loss. This makes licorice creams an effective treatment for dry skin, providing long-lasting hydration and comfort to parched skin, leaving it feeling soft, supple, and healthy-looking.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: Helps to lock in moisture and soothe dry, irritated skin by reducing inflammation and improving skin hydration.
Triterpenoids: Possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to calm redness and irritation caused by dry skin, while also protecting the skin from environmental stressors.
Saponins: Have emollient and humectant properties, allowing them to moisturize and hydrate the skin, while also helping to repair the skin's natural barrier function.
remedy_parts: Roots: Licorice root has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making it effective in soothing and moisturizing dry skin.
Barks: Licorice bark contains flavonoids and phenolic acids that help to improve skin hydration, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 200g of dried glycyrrhiza glabra root and grind it into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle for 3 minutes.
Combine the powdered glycyrrhiza glabra with 100g of beeswax and 50g of coconut oil in a double boiler and heat until the beeswax is fully melted after 10 minutes.
Stir in 20g of vitamin e oil and 20g of shea butter into the mixture and continue heating for an additional 5 minutes or until the ingredients are well combined.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool to around 60°c before whipping with a hand mixer for 10 minutes until it reaches a creamy consistency.
Pour the cream into sterilized jars and store them in the refrigerator at a temperature of 4°c or below, using within 6 weeks of preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel creams helps with dry skin because of its unique properties that moisturize, soothe, and nourish the skin. The fennel extract in these creams is rich in antioxidants and omega-3 fatty acids, which work to repair and restore the skin's natural barrier function. Additionally, fennel's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce redness and irritation associated with dry skin, leaving the skin feeling soft, supple, and refreshed. As a result, fennel creams provide long-lasting hydration and comfort to parched skin, making them an effective solution for those struggling with dry, rough, or itchy skin.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Fennel cream's antioxidant properties help protect the skin from environmental stressors, such as pollution and UV radiation, which can contribute to dryness and irritation.
Moisturizing and emollient properties: The fatty acids and triglycerides present in fennel oil help lock in moisture and soothe dry, irritated skin, reducing flakiness and itchiness.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Fennel's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce redness and swelling associated with dry skin conditions, such as eczema and psoriasis, making it a valuable ingredient for soothing and calming the skin.
remedy_parts: Roots: Fennel roots have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in soothing and calming dry, irritated skin.
Seeds: Fennel seeds contain oils rich in antioxidants and fatty acids, which help to moisturize and nourish dry skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh foeniculum vulgare leaves and clean them thoroughly under running water.
Combine one cup of dried foeniculum vulgare leaves with two cups of distilled water in a heat-resistant glass container.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 20 minutes to infuse the herbal properties into the liquid.
Strain the infused liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve and discard the solids.
Mix one tablespoon of beeswax with four tablespoons of coconut oil and add the infused foeniculum vulgare liquid to create a smooth, emollient cream.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Burdock creams helps with dry skin because of its exceptional emollient and soothing properties. The root extract in burdock contains natural oils that deeply moisturize and nourish the skin, leaving it feeling soft, supple, and hydrated. It also helps to lock in moisture, reducing the appearance of fine lines and wrinkles. Additionally, burdock's anti-inflammatory agents calm irritated skin, reducing redness and discomfort associated with dry skin conditions.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: Burdock root's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties help reduce redness, irritation, and inflammation associated with dry skin, promoting a smoother and more even complexion.

Moisturizing and emollient properties: The saponins in burdock cream create a protective barrier on the skin, locking in moisture and reducing water loss, leaving dry skin feeling soft, supple, and hydrated.

Wound-healing and regenerative properties: Burdock's wound-healing compounds help stimulate collagen production, promote tissue regeneration, and accelerate the healing process of dry, cracked skin, reducing the appearance of fine lines and wrinkles.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in mucilages and antioxidants, burdock roots help soothe and moisturize dry skin.
Seeds: High in essential fatty acids and vitamins, burdock seeds nourish and hydrate dry skin, promoting its natural barrier function.
remedy_recipe: Harvest arctium lappa roots and leaves, rinsing them gently with cold water to remove impurities.
Dry the plant material in a well-ventilated area or using a dehydrator for 24 hours at 50°c temperature.
Grind 20 grams of dried arctium lappa roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle, taking care not to contaminate the mixture.
Combine the powdered root with 100 milliliters of cold-pressed sweet almond oil and 10 millilitres of beeswax in a double boiler, heating the mixture until the wax is fully incorporated.
Stir the cream for 30 minutes as it cools and thickens, then transfer it to sterilized glass jars, storing them in the refrigerator for up to six months.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for dry skin are topical products infused with natural herbs and botanicals that provide long-lasting relief from dry, itchy, and flaky skin. These creams help to lock in moisture, soothe irritation, and promote healthy skin barrier function. Examples of herbal creams include those containing aloe vera, chamomile, and green tea, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and improve hydration levels. By using these creams, individuals with dry skin can experience significant improvements in their daily lives, such as reduced discomfort and embarrassment from dry, cracked skin, allowing them to feel more confident and comfortable in their own skin.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat dry skin with herbal creams is by choosing rich and nourishing formulations that contain hydrating herbs like aloe vera, coconut oil, and shea butter. Look for creams that are specifically designed to lock in moisture and soothe dryness. Apply the cream immediately after bathing or showering while the skin is still damp, allowing it to absorb fully into the skin.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to dry skin that are treated with herbal creams are eczema, psoriasis, and dermatitis. Herbal creams rich in emollients, vitamins, and antioxidants help soothe and moisturize irritated skin, reducing inflammation and discomfort. Ingredients like aloe vera, chamomile, and calendula have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that promote healing and relief from itching and redness. These natural remedies can provide effective alternative treatments for various skin conditions without harsh chemicals or side effects.
